question: I found a LumenLoop article. How should I identify which directory projects that specific content item mentions?

golden.answer: Resolve a concrete content result first, preserve its numeric id and content type, then call get_related_projects with both values. Report only the returned directory projects. This relation is content → projects; do not substitute a general keyword search or reverse it into project → content.
keyFacts: Uses a concrete content id plus its content type with get_related_projects.
Treats the result as projects mentioned by that content item.
avoid: Do NOT claim every project matching the article's keywords was mentioned by the article.
Do NOT reverse the relation into content about a project.
golden.notes: Behavioral service-contract case; returned project identities are live data.

sources.note: Fetch-verified local path exists and defines the exercised contract/workflow.

evidence: 2026-07-11 localhost:8787 search→execute probe observed: HTTP 200; a semantic content seed (article 8290) resolved through get_related_projects to an ok envelope with one mentioned project.
catalog/manifest.json exists and supports the behavioral golden.
Sibling sweep: reviewed existing battery cases sharing the exercised surface; no contradictory behavioral golden found.
